summary: Story 1.6 is implementation-ready for the vertical month-grouped timeline home. It is source-backed, tenant-safe, scoped to the timeline-home slice, and gives @dev enough implementation and testing context without expanding into period creation, media delivery, filters, sharing, or admin surfaces.

template_compliance.notes: Required story sections are present and populated: Status, Executor Assignment, Story, Acceptance Criteria, Integration Verification, CodeRabbit Integration, Tasks / Subtasks, Dev Notes, Testing, Change Log, Dev Agent Record, and QA Results. Only future-owned Dev Agent Record and QA Results placeholders remain, which is normal before implementation.

executor_assignment.notes: Executor @dev and quality_gate @architect are known, distinct agents. quality_gate_tools is non-empty and aligned to the feature/service/UI route slice.

dependency_sequence.notes: Dependencies on Stories 1.1, 1.2, 1.4a, 1.4b, 1.4c, and 1.5 are explicit. Story 1.5 has a PASS gate and the DevOps handoff reports PR #11 merged; the older textual status mismatch in Story 1.5 is noted as non-blocking.

file_structure.notes: The story identifies the correct Laravel memory product locations: app/Services/MemoryTimelineService.php, app/Livewire/Memory/MemoryTimeline.php, resources/views/livewire/memory/memory-timeline.blade.php, routes/web.php, translations, and focused PHPUnit test paths.

ui_frontend_completeness.notes: The UI guidance is specific enough for implementation: vertical month separators, readable cards, responsive wrapping, stable dimensions, accessible detail action, no tables/admin/Filament/marketing/blog-card/masonry/map/complex animation treatment, and a diary-oriented empty state.

acceptance_criteria_coverage.notes: Tasks map to all six acceptance criteria and all three integration verification points, including ordering by timeline date, month grouping, tenant scoping, empty state, card metadata, and cross-tenant exclusion.

testing_guidance.notes: The story requires focused PHPUnit coverage for service grouping/order/cross-tenant behavior and Livewire rendering/empty-state/detail-link behavior, plus route/profile regression tests, route inspection, and Pint.

security_scope.notes: Tenant membership, route tenant scoping, non-leaking cross-tenant exclusion, no public media URLs, and no private path exposure are explicit in AC/IV, tasks, scope boundaries, and CodeRabbit focus areas.

coderabbit_integration.notes: CodeRabbit is enabled in core-config.yaml and the story contains story type analysis, specialized agents, required quality gate tasks, self-healing behavior, and focus areas matching frontend/API/security risk.

code_intelligence.notes: isCodeIntelAvailable returned false in this session, so duplicate detection was skipped according to the validate-next-story fallback rule.

anti_hallucination.notes: Technical claims were cross-checked against PRD Story 1.6, architecture timeline/service guidance, UI architecture state ownership and route guidance, coding standards, current routes/component/model/view, Story 1.5 gate, and the DevOps-to-SM Story 1.6 handoff.

dev_readiness.notes: The story is self-contained enough for @dev to implement without inventing requirements or reading beyond the cited artifacts for normal codebase conventions.

nice_to_have: Reconcile Story 1.5's textual status from Ready for Review to the final lifecycle status in a separate closeout step; do not block Story 1.6 because the PASS gate and DevOps handoff already establish the implementation baseline.
During implementation, keep photo count conditional until an authorized media/count boundary exists, as the story correctly avoids adding media delivery scope.
